title: End-to-end wire-up + smoke test
acceptance_criteria: src/index.ts wires the full pipeline: creates Pool + Redis client; creates VirtualClock (in SIMULATE mode) or RealClock; creates MarketDataSimulator or Fyers/Angel One adapter via broker-factory; creates StraddleCalculator, VixFeed, EntryEngine, PaperTradeExecutor, PositionMonitor; starts broker → starts StraddleCalculator → starts PositionMonitor → starts EntryEngine → starts Fastify server; handles SIGTERM gracefully (stop all, close DB, close Redis)
In SIMULATE=true mode, VirtualClock is used and advanced at SIM_TICK_INTERVAL_MS; the simulator is wired to the VirtualClock so time advances deterministically

src/test/integration/smoke.test.ts: (1) creates VirtualClock starting at 09:14 IST; (2) starts full pipeline with VirtualClock; (3) generates ticks via VirtualClock.advance() to 09:17 IST; (4) asserts that at least one paper_trades row exists in DB with status='open' or status='closed'; (5) confirms that WS /ws/ticks received at least one straddle broadcast; (6) advances VirtualClock to 15:25 IST; (7) confirms all open positions are now closed (EOD exit); (8) verifies gross_pnl and net_pnl are non-null and computed as strings (decimal.js format)
The smoke test uses the integration test DB (not dev DB) and clears state before/after

README.md adds a 'Quick Start' section: docker compose up -d → bun run migrate → SIMULATE=true bun run sim → open http://localhost:3000 in browser (Fastify serves static frontend from frontend/dist/ in prod mode; in dev run frontend separately with bun run dev in frontend/)
bun run sim from the repo root starts the full pipeline in simulate mode without errors
output_format: code plus plain English explanation of every non-obvious decision
